channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: US Imposes New Iran-Related Sanctions Targeting Entities, Individuals and Tankers
item.description: The US Treasury Department announced new sanctions targeting Iranian entities, individuals, and LPG tankers as tensions remain high amid stalled negotiations.

item.title: Macron Plans E3 Format Meeting With Zelensky to Structure Peace Talks
item.description: French President Macron announced plans to meet Zelensky in an E3 format to organize support within the coalition of the willing and structure future peace negotiations.

item.title: EU Leaders Push Faster Expansion at Balkan Summit in Montenegro
item.description: European leaders used a summit in Montenegro to accelerate EU membership prospects for Balkan nations as enlargement gains strategic urgency.

item.title: Ilhan Omar Only Democrat to Vote Against House Ukraine Aid, Russia Sanctions Package
item.description: Rep. Ilhan Omar was the sole Democrat voting against a House bill providing Ukraine aid and imposing additional Russia sanctions, highlighting divisions within the party.

item.title: Twelve Democrats Vote Against House Armed Services Committee NDAA
item.description: A dozen Democrats opposed the HASC nearly $1.15 trillion version of the annual defense policy bill, marking a rare committee split.

item.title: Xi Jinping to visit North Korea next week in first trip since 2019
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries announced Friday, in what will be his first visit since 2019. The meeting comes amid shifting regional dynamics and US-China tensions.

item.title: Trump urges firing of intelligence community employees in major shake-up
item.description: President Donald Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants acting DNI Bill Pulte to begin firing large numbers of intelligence community employees as part of a broad shake-up of US intelligence agencies.

item.title: Hong Kong shares of HSBC, Standard Chartered fall on China capital controls
item.description: Hong Kong-listed shares of HSBC, Standard Chartered and AIA fell sharply on concerns Beijing tighter capital controls could dent business of global financial firms with mainland China exposure.

item.title: SpaceX IPO bars investors from China and Hong Kong
item.description: SpaceX underwriters have barred investors in China and Hong Kong from participating in the planned IPO, Bloomberg reported, in a move reflecting ongoing US-China tensions.

item.title: US officials discuss government stakes in AI companies
item.description: Senior US officials held preliminary discussions with major AI companies about the potential for government to buy shares in their firms, NOTUS reported, reflecting growing national security concerns around AI development.

item.title: Azerbaijani Crew Members Killed in Ukraine Drone Attack on Cargo Ships
item.description: Russia accused Ukraine of killing Azerbaijani crew members aboard two cargo vessels headed for a Russian port in a drone attack in the Sea of Azov. Baku confirmed at least five of its citizens were killed in the strikes.

item.title: US Forces Board Sanctioned Tanker in Indian Ocean
item.description: U.S. forces conducted an interdiction of the stateless oil tanker Davina in the Indian Ocean, enforcing sanctions amid ongoing tensions with Iran over maritime security.

item.title: WFP Warns Middle East Conflict Pushing Millions Toward Hunger
item.description: The UN World Food Programme warned that the Middle East conflict is driving millions closer to hunger, with rising fuel and transport costs inflating food prices while aid agencies face funding shortfalls.

item.title: China Tracks Dutch Warship Transiting Taiwan Strait
item.description: China's military dispatched naval and air assets to track and monitor the Dutch frigate HNLMS De Ruyter as it transited the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions in the strategically vital waterway.

item.title: Former Tibet Government Head Sentenced to Life for Corruption
item.description: Che Dalha, the former head of the Tibet Autonomous Region government, was sentenced to life in prison for corruption in a high-profile crackdown on graft, China's official Xinhua news agency reported.

item.title: Lebanese PM urges Iran to stop using south Lebanon as leverage in US talks
item.description: Prime Minister Nawaf Salam warned that southern Lebanon should not be used as a bargaining chip as Iran negotiates with Washington.

item.title: Finland blocks property purchases linked to Chinese network over security concerns
item.description: Finnish authorities halted property acquisitions after determining sites were located in strategically sensitive areas.

item.title: Bulgaria PM warns Europe cannot defeat nuclear-armed Russia, calls for diplomacy
item.description: Rumen Radev told EU-Western Balkans summit that Europe cannot achieve conventional victory against the worlds largest nuclear power.

item.title: Associate of Manchester synagogue attacker admits separate terror plot
item.description: An Islamic extremist pleaded guilty to planning an attack on the UK Defence Academy with the man who later killed two people at a Manchester synagogue.

item.title: Maritime Drone Explodes at Romanian Black Sea Port
item.description: A Ukrainian sea drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Constanta port without causing casualties, with Ukraine saying Russia jammed the vessel causing it to drift off course.

item.title: China Military Tracks Dutch Frigate in Taiwan Strait
item.description: China dispatched naval and air assets to monitor Dutch frigate HNLMS De Ruyter transiting the Taiwan Strait, the second such incident involving the warship after Beijing accused it of illegally entering Paracel Islands waters last week.

item.title: Over 1,000 Nigerians Seek Repatriation from South Africa After Xenophobic Attacks
item.description: At least 1,094 Nigerians have registered for voluntary return from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, a sharp increase from 130 previously seeking repatriation, Nigeria's government said.

item.title: Somalia Restores Order After Capital Fighting Between Government and Opposition Militias
item.description: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and opposition-allied militias exchanged fire, forcing civilians to flee the capital.

item.title: ECB Expected to Hike Rates Next Week as Iran War Fuels Inflation Pressures
item.description: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates, becoming the first major central bank to do so since the Iran conflict unleashed an energy crisis stoking inflationary pressures in the euro zone.

item.title: IAEA Negotiates Sixth Ceasefire to Repair Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Power Line
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency brokered a temporary local ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia to allow repairs to a critical power supply line at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear facility, marking the sixth such negotiated pause since late last year.
